articles.title: Claude Cowork expands to mobile and web
articles.description: Anthropic’s Claude Cowork is now available on web and mobile for Max subscribers. Until now, Cowork largely lived on a user’s laptop. With the update, users can start a task from their desk, get status updates on their phone, and pick up the finished output later — even if their laptop is closed.

articles.title: X adds a video editor to encourage creators to post original content, not stolen reposts
articles.description: X is rolling out a new video editor and recorder for iOS with multilingual captions, green-screen effects, and other editing tools.

articles.title: Chemistry Ventures is raising $500M for its second fund
articles.description: Chemistry Ventures, the VC firm launched by Bessemer, Index Ventures, and Andreessen Horowitz alums, is raising $500M for its second fund.

articles.title: AI law startup Norm raises $120M, hits unicorn valuation
articles.description: AI law startup Norm has raised a $120 million Series C round led by Khosla Ventures, valuing the startup at $1.2 billion.

articles.title: This startup pits dealerships against each other to bid on your used car
articles.description: Bidbus, which lets dealerships bid on used cars, has raised $15 million in a Series A round that was led by early-stage mobility fund Ibex Investors.

articles.title: Hacktivists call out Trump by hacking and defacing US Army websites
articles.description: The U.S. Army has fixed two of its websites that were hacked to display messages calling President Trump a "pedophile" and a "thief."

articles.title: Savi’s app aims to protect consumers from realistic AI scams like kidnappers demanding ransom
articles.description: The company just raised $7 million in seed funding, and is launching its app for iPhone and Android on Tuesday.

articles.title: The first American autonomous ground vehicles are fighting in Ukraine
articles.description: Forterra has deployed more than 100 of its self-driving ATVs in conflict zones in Ukraine.

articles.title: Netflix invented binge-watching. Now it may have outgrown it.
articles.description: A new report suggests Netflix viewers aren’t sticking around for Season 2. The bigger issue may be that binge-watching itself is no longer the advantage it once was.

articles.title: The ‘first’ AI-run ransomware attack still needed a human
articles.description: An AI agent carried out the technical execution of a real-world ransomware attack for the first known time, but new details show a human still chose the victim, set up the infrastructure, and supplied stolen credentials — meaning it wasn't quite the fully autonomous cybercrime debut that last week's headlines suggested.

articles.title: US investors will soon get access to SK Hynix, another memory maker riding the AI boom
articles.description: SK Hynix is experiencing a boom credited to AI. It will ride that to a multibillion-dollar U.S. IPO, expected to take place on Friday.

articles.title: Vercel CEO Guillermo Rauch on the fight to split off models from agents
articles.description: "The reality is, when you're optimizing for production, you start looking at a price/performance," Guillermo Rauch tells TechCrunch.

articles.title: You can now customize Siri’s pace and expressivity in the latest iOS 27 beta
articles.description: The update is part of Apple's broader effort to make Siri feel more natural and personal, as it rebuilds the assistant around generative AI.

articles.title: Every major tech layoff in 2026 that has name-checked AI
articles.description: A running look — in reverse chronological order — at the bigger tech companies that have announced significant layoffs this year with AI as a stated factor.

articles.title: Amazon competitor Bookshop.org says Kobo eReader support will happen this year after all
articles.description: Bookship.org seemed to delay this anticipated partnership again, but tells TechCrunch that it has settled business terms and is working on integration.

articles.title: If you use Google, you’re training its AI. Here’s how to opt out.
articles.description: Consider this a belated PSA: A recent change to Google’s privacy settings is allowing the company to store more of your data, including media such as “images, files, and audio and video recordings,” to improve its AI models.

articles.title: Apple brings back card payments for Apple Account purchases in India after a four-year hiatus
articles.description: Apple has started a phased rollout of card payments for Apple Account purchases in India after adapting to the country's payments framework.

articles.title: Microsoft lays off nearly 5,000 employees across Xbox, commercial sales
articles.description: Microsoft cut around 4,800 roles, or 2.1% of its global workforce, on Monday — the latest in a series of layoffs that’s stoking fears of AI replacing jobs. The layoffs will hit Xbox and commercial sales the hardest.

articles.title: Reddit is using LLMs to solve a problem LLMs largely created
articles.description: In the AI era, platforms have no choice but to fight fire with fire to cull spam.

articles.title: Canadian spy agency says it hacked drug traffickers, extremists, and a ransomware gang last year
articles.description: The hacking operations disclosed in a Canadian spy agency's annual report underscores some pressing national security threats facing the country and its top allies.

articles.title: New virus catalog reveals which pathogens pose the greatest threat
articles.description: The data can help predict what a future pandemic virus might look like.

articles.title: ULA's last six Atlas Vs can't launch anything besides Boeing's Starliner
articles.description: Amazon says it has enough satellites in orbit to begin initial broadband service at mid-latitudes later this year.

articles.title: How AI could enable autonomous robot workers in workplaces—and maybe homes
articles.description: Top robotics researchers and founders explain how robot autonomy is evolving.

articles.title: FCC to end Biden-era rule that forces ISPs to list all their fees
articles.description: FCC to let ISPs stop listing all passthrough fees, give single "up to" price.

articles.title: Kremlin suspected of flying drones over Europe using Russian shadow fleet
articles.description: Drone intruders that possibly flew from Russian ships showed Europe isn’t ready.

articles.title: What is the oldest American object ever launched into space?
articles.description: From a Revolutionary War flag to the Statue of Liberty...

articles.title: NRC is (sort of) getting rid of "as low as reasonably achievable" standard
articles.description: Its issues with current nuclear safety standards are termed semantic, not physical.

articles.title: Katalyst's satellite rescue mission is now in pursuit of NASA's Swift
articles.description: It will take several weeks for the Link spacecraft to rendezvous with NASA's Swift observatory.

articles.title: Secret Claude tracker shocks users after Anthropic’s anti-surveillance stance
articles.description: Anthropic accused of spying on users; engineer says “experiment” is over.

articles.title: The incredible shrinking Xbox: Five studios, 3,200 employees let go
articles.description: Move affects ~20% of the gaming division, which will refocus on its biggest franchises.

articles.title: F1 in Britain: Automated software to blame for crushing expectations
articles.description: Sometimes races finish behind a safety car, but it's not always satisfying.

articles.title: There were not one, but two asteroid encounters this weekend
articles.description: The Torifune asteroid turns out to be shaped like a peanut.

articles.title: UK regulator warns of "arms race" to keep up with AI use in financial services
articles.description: FCA official makes case for greater powers for watchdog as millions use technology for personal finance decisions.

articles.title: Bentley teases its first EV, the Torcal
articles.description: The new model will be officially unveiled in late September.

articles.title: The Czinger 21C might be the wildest car we drive all year
articles.description: This hybrid V8 has organic-looking 3D-printed components and shatters lap records.

articles.title: Chemical accidents rise as Trump administration proposes weakening safety rules
articles.description: Chemicals from accidents that injured or killed people increased by nearly 50 percent in recent years.

articles.title: The missing 500 million: Cosmic bombardment melted Earth's first crust
articles.description: The heat of the Hadean may have come from impacts as well as the interior.

articles.title: Review: Supergirl is not the disaster its low box office suggests
articles.description: It’s a pretty good movie, but it needed to be a great movie to thrive in an oversaturated superhero market.

articles.title: Better Auth is joining Vercel
articles.description: Article URL: https://better-auth.com/blog/better-auth-joins-vercel Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48819512 Points: 36 # Comments: 14

articles.title: Microsoft Fire IdTech Team at Id Software
articles.description: Article URL: https://gamefromscratch.com/microsoft-fire-idtech-team-at-id-software/ Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48819244 Points: 147 # Comments: 93

tech.summary:
今日科技动态摘要

1. **AI与安全领域新进展**：AI法律初创公司Norm完成1.2亿美元融资，估值达独角兽级别；Savi推出防AI诈骗应用，旨在保护用户免受逼真的勒索诈骗；首个由AI驱动的勒索软件攻击仍需要人类参与，揭示AI在网络安全中的局限性。  
2. **消费科技与平台更新**：Claude Cowork扩展至移动端和网页端；X平台新增视频编辑器，鼓励创作者发布原创内容而非搬运；iOS 27测试版允许用户自定义Siri语速和表现力。  
3. **汽车与硬件市场动态**：新创平台让经销商竞拍用户二手车；美国投资者即将获得SK海力士（AI热潮中的又一家存储芯片厂商）投资渠道；美国首批自动驾驶地面车辆已在乌克兰投入实战。  
4. **流媒体与出版业变化**：Netflix虽开创“刷剧”模式，但可能已超越该模式；Bookshop.org确认今年将支持Kobo电子阅读器，挑战亚马逊。  
5. **行业趋势与风险**：2026年多家科技公司大规模裁员与AI关联；黑客组织攻击并篡改美国陆军网站，点名特朗普；Vercel CEO讨论拆分AI模型与智能体的行业争议。  

**今日最值得关注的发展**：AI法律及安全领域融资活跃，同时AI驱动的网络攻击与防御技术同步升级，凸显技术双刃剑效应。

ai.summary:
今日AI动态摘要

1. **Hugging Face多项更新**：推出Foundry托管计算服务、LeRobot v0.6.0（新增“想象、评估、改进”功能）、PRX数据策略第四部分、Kernels重大更新，并与Cerebras合作将Gemma 4应用于实时语音AI。
2. **Google AI与教育结合**：纽约市教育工作者和行业领袖在Google办公室共商AI课堂应用；Google AI博客还预告了2026年6月的最新AI动态。
3. **具身智能与机器人视觉突破**：开源多模态Skill范式Skill-Omni、首个空间原生的具身视觉基模、蚂蚁灵波空间感知模型LingBot-Depth 2.0发布，以及科技沙龙聚焦具身智能产业化的“三大困惑”。
4. **AI科研与产业争议**：DeepSeek招聘被“华为天才少年”公开吐槽“面到最不专业”；WAIC 2026科学智能讨论AI4S从“辅助计算”转向“自主发现”，中国如何重塑全球科研版图。
5. **企业动态与基准测试**：ScarfBench发布针对企业Java框架迁移的AI智能体基准测试；国内造车新势力被评价为“中国最神奇的造车新势力”。

**今日最值得关注的发展**：Hugging Face与Cerebras合作将Gemma 4推向实时语音AI，以及具身智能领域多项视觉与技能模型的开源发布，标志着AI在感知与交互能力上迈出关键一步。
